name: MATLAB
description: A numerical computing environment optimised for matrix operations, signal processing, and engineering simulations. Ubiquitous in experimental physics and engineering.
whyUseIt: MATLAB excels at matrix computations, data analysis, and creating publication-quality plots. Its extensive toolboxes cover signal processing, image analysis, control systems, and more. Many physics labs use MATLAB for data acquisition and analysis.
fieldPosition: Experimental and computational physics — standard tool in many research labs and universities
physicistWorkflow: Load experimental data, clean and process it, fit models, produce plots for publication.
supervisorExpects: Proficiency in MATLAB scripting, matrix operations, and data visualisation.
computationalImpact: Accelerates data analysis and prototyping of numerical algorithms. Matrix-native syntax makes linear algebra intuitive.
demoDescription: Load a set of oscilloscope traces, apply FFT to identify frequency components, fit a decaying sinusoid, and extract the Q-factor of a resonant circuit.

vocabulary.definition: The set of variables currently in memory
An add-on package providing specialised functionality

beginnerMistakes: Using loops instead of vectorised operations
Not pre-allocating arrays before filling them
